items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:
Candice Carty-Williams, Patrick Freyne and Guardian readers discuss the titles they have read over the last month. Join the conversation in the comments

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Maria Beatrix Anna Frances of Austria-Este was a high aristocrat from the Austria-Este branch of the House of Habsburg.
items.body: As daughter to the ruling Duke of Modena she was born archduchess of Austria-Este and princess of Modena. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Crompton Parkinson was a British electrical manufacturing company.
items.body: It was formed in 1927 by the merger of Crompton & Co. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Main Uralian Fault (MUF) is a fault that runs north–south through the middle of the Ural Mountains for over 2,000 km.
items.body: It separates both Europe from Asia and the three, or four, western megazones of the Urals from the three eastern megazones: namely the Pre-Uralian Foredeep, West Uralian, and the Central Uralian to the west, and the Tagil-Magnitogorskian, East Uralian, and Tra

items.hook: World War II: During the Beisfjord massacre in Norway, 15 Norwegian paramilitary guards help members of the SS to kill 288 political prisoners from Yugoslavia.
items.body: World War II, or the Second World War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: At least seven people are killed and 32 others are injured after a bomb explodes on an Israeli tour bus at Burgas Airport, Bulgaria.
items.body: The 2012 Burgas bus bombing was a terrorist attack carried out by a suicide bomber on a passenger bus transporting Israeli tourists at the Burgas Airport in Burgas, Bulgaria, on 18 July 2012. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant requires Christians to either accept dhimmi status, emigrate from ISIL lands, or be killed.
items.body: The Islamic State (IS), also known as the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL), the Islamic State of Iraq and Syria (ISIS), and by its Arabic acronym Daesh, is a transnational Salafi jihadist militant organisation and internationally unrecognised quasi-state. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: A Canadair CL-44 and Sukhoi Su-15 collide in mid-air near Yerevan, Armenia, killing four.
items.body: The Canadair CL-44 was a Canadian turboprop airliner and cargo aircraft based on the Bristol Britannia that was developed and produced by Canadair in the late 1950s and early 1960s. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The attraction is often compared to a combination of skydiving, bungee jumping, and hang gliding.
items.body: Skycoaster is the name of a free-fall and flight-simulating amusement park attraction produced and managed by Skycoaster Company, LLC.. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

Almost 14% of Ireland is bog: vast swathes of moss-carpeted land, below which layers of ancient history have

items.hook: Jason Matthew Richwine is an American political commentator and author.

items.hook: Mark Alsop is an Australian DJ and remixer.
items.body: Alsop is one of the longest-continuous working DJs in Australia. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The bishop of Florence blesses the first foundation stone for the new campanile (bell tower) of the Florence Cathedral, designed by the artist Giotto di Bondone.
items.body: Florence is the capital and most populous city of the Italian region of Tuscany, and, as of 2026, with a population of 361,625, the 8th most populous city in the country. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: ESA’s Mars Express meets NASA’s Psyche
‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: The First Vatican Council decrees the dogma of papal infallibility.
items.body: The First Ecumenical Council of the Vatican, commonly known as the First Vatican Council or Vatican I, was the 20th ecumenical council of the Catholic Church, held three centuries after the preceding Council of Trent which was adjourned in 1563. The world is stranger than it lets on.
